assertions.title: Release check passes (npm run release-check exits with code 0)

steps.expected: All validation steps pass, exit code 0
steps.observed: Exit code 0 but secret-scan step reports false positives

steps.action: Run npm run secret-scan to investigate
steps.expected: Secret scan passes with no findings
steps.observed: Secret scan fails with 9 personal absolute path matches in .factory/validation/

steps.expected: No personal paths in actual source code
steps.observed: Source code is clean - only match is in secret-scan.sh itself (placeholder filtering)

steps.expected: Files are validation artifacts, not source code
steps.observed: All 9 matches are in .factory/validation/ directory - validation reports and test isolation contexts

assertions.issues: FALSE POSITIVE: Secret-scan.sh does not exclude .factory/validation/ directory, causing false positives on validation artifacts that intentionally contain personal absolute paths for test isolation context. Actual source code is clean with no PII or secrets. All 9 matches are in .factory/validation/ subdirectories (documentation/, governance/, security/) and represent validation report metadata, not source code.

frictions.description: secret-scan.sh has no exclusion for .factory/validation/ directory despite it being intentionally committed per CLAUDE.md
frictions.resolution: Requires updating scripts/secret-scan.sh to add ':(exclude).factory/validation' to the git grep command

frictions.description: Validation reports use absolute paths for isolation context which triggers secret-scan false positives
frictions.resolution: Consider using relative paths in validation reports or excluding the entire .factory/validation/ directory from secret-scan

summary: VAL-TEST-003 fails due to false positives from validation artifacts in .factory/validation/ directory. The npm run release-check command exits with code 0 (technically passing the release check), but the secret-scan step reports 9 personal absolute path matches. Investigation confirmed all matches are in .factory/validation/ subdirectories and represent validation report metadata with test isolation contexts, not actual source code. Source code verification excluding .factory/validation/ showed the actual codebase is clean with no PII or secrets. This is a pre-existing infrastructure issue where secret-scan.sh does not exclude the .factory/validation/ directory, which is intentionally committed per CLAUDE.md. The assertion status is 'fail' due to the false positive, but the actual release readiness criteria (clean source code) is satisfied.
